topic: oak lignin breakdown and vanilla aroma

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Oak is seasoned and toasted before a barrel is used for fermentation or maturation.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Heat and seasoning break down part of the wood's lignin and create aromatic aldehydes including vanillin, which can then be extracted into wine.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine may gain a vanilla aroma alongside other oak-derived characters, with intensity depending on oak origin, toast, age and contact.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: lignin breakdown contributes vanillin, helping explain vanilla notes from oak.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
